essence: David is frustrated that handover documents list multiple options without declaring the chosen next action, leaving the next context window unable to decide; he wants the format changed to state the next action explicitly and mark the rest as future plans, plus the handover instructions updated.

actions.text: Update handover format and instructions: explicitly name the chosen next action, mark other options as future plans
actions.target: handover-pattern skill / Claude Code handover instructions

loop.label: handover format: name the next action

essence: David declines a quick touchbase call and instead directs the session to run the migration verification protocol on the test migration work.

David plans how to run work asynchronously across AI contexts — weighing multiple separate context windows vs one window with background agents, with a central plan plus per-item requirement docs — decides to start with Telegram as the only first channel, and goes with the recommended option A after considering a strategic-pause-plus-handover.

actions.text: Start comms integration with Telegram only; document other channel options for later
Proceed with option A (recommended approach) for async multi-agent execution

essence: David directs the test-migration session to use the librarian to check learnings and verify tests are genuinely migrated from the old system rather than invented for coverage's sake, asks why the suite isn't yet at A+ grade, and wants Lisa's health-check commands run to find what's broken.

actions.text: Verify tests were truly migrated from old system, not invented; focus on fixing what was broken

essence: David accepts the session's recommendation, raises a second topic of where comms apps fit (three listed so far), and asks how much longer the phase-three background process will run.
David confirms phase three doesn't need to stay in the current conversation — everything required is prepared, so it can be handed off to a background agent that reports back.

actions.text: Hand phase three continuation off to a background agent and await its feedback

essence: David commits to option A, the rolling-task management technique, at roughly six hours a day, with a handover coming for the next task and a check that nothing beyond the existing handover document needs updating.

essence: David asks the session to load the original conversation and produce a handover for the next batch, noting the task list stands at nine done and one open and questioning whether ten is a cap or more batches are coming for this problem set.

essence: A friend (voice message) says they're nervously trying improv comedy this week with visiting friends from Propaganda and proposes meeting David next Tuesday, awaiting confirmation.
David asks whether anything learned in the session warrants an update to the KDD, a routine end-of-session knowledge-capture check.
David asks whether any patterns or learnings from the last session should be carried forward — the same routine pattern-recognition check that feeds the KDD.

David briefs the next batch of work with two pieces of information: a report that the knowledge-driven development area has been refactored into better categorization and smaller groupings, and a handover (via a prepared ChatGist document) so execution can proceed.

essence: David decides documentation answers to open questions should be officially recorded with links, and that replicated parts of the current data mesh can be compressed down to summaries — as long as the historical rationale for past decisions is preserved, with verification before summarizing.

essence: David steers the migration session: capture session learnings, note that migration documents are getting large, search systems for related stories/issues/backlogs, warns a parallel conversation was modifying files (now finished), cancels a previously requested commit, and asks for a handover to the next conversation.

actions.text: Do NOT perform the previously requested commit

essence: A peer conversation about building wrappers around the BMAD method for visualization and action capabilities — one party describes pairing an 'astrocyte' with context engineering in their own framework and floats collaborating on vMAD-method side projects, agreeing to connect later in the week.
